title: Leisure and sports managers and proprietors
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate the management or operation of farms, ranches, greenhouses, aquacultural operations, nurseries, timber tracts, or other agricultural establishments. May hire, train, and supervise farm workers or contract for services to carry out the day-to-day activities of the managed operation. May engage in or supervise planting, cultivating, harvesting, and financial and marketing activities. Plan, direct, or coordinate gambling operations in a casino. May formulate house rules. Plan, direct, or coordinate entertainment and recreational activities and operations of a recreational facility, including cruise ships and parks. (Based on 14 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Negotiate with buyers for the sale, storage, or shipment of crops or livestock.
Hire, supervise, and train support workers and evaluate their performance.
Direct crop production operations, such as planning, tilling, planting, fertilizing, cultivating, spraying, and harvesting.
Inspect facilities and equipment for signs of disrepair and perform necessary maintenance work.
Monitor activities, such as irrigation, chemical application, harvesting, and grading, to ensure adherence to safety regulations or standards.
Prepare reports required by central and devolved government laws and maintain financial, operational, production, or employment records for farms or ranches.
Plan programs of events or schedules of activities and direct the preparation and submission of regulatory agency applications or correspondence.
Resolve customer complaints regarding problems, such as payout errors, and oversee customer inquiries or services rendered.
Conduct inspections to determine crop maturity or condition or detect disease or insect infestation.
Supervise the construction of farm or ranch structures and direct the development or implementation of policies related to compliance.
Advise retail managers on the development of loss-investigation procedures and compliance with applicable codes, laws, and standards.
Train loss prevention staff, retail managers, or store employees on loss control, prevention measures, and compliance-related topics or procedures.
Supervise surveillance, detection, or criminal processing related to theft and conduct internal investigations of employee theft or policy violations.
Coordinate theft and fraud investigations involving organized group activities and support law enforcement partnerships in such matters.
Provide assistance to internal or external auditors during compliance reviews and maintain documentation of compliance activities.
Develop and maintain partnerships with federal, state, local law enforcement, and the retail loss prevention community to enhance security.
Track attendance, participation, or performance data related to wellness events and evaluate fitness and wellness programs for effectiveness.
Organize and oversee events such as organized runs or walks and develop or coordinate fitness and wellness programs or services.
Demonstrate proper operation of fitness equipment, such as cardio machines and resistance machines, to improve participant fitness.
Manage or oversee fitness or recreation facilities, ensuring safe and clean environments, as well as maintenance of equipment.
Supervise fitness or wellness specialists and conduct or facilitate training sessions for staff on wellness and fitness topics.
Develop marketing campaigns to promote healthy lifestyles and participation in fitness or wellness programs and events.
Identify potential for loss in retail operations and develop strategies to eliminate it, including monitoring compliance with safety and inventory control procedures.
Analyze retail data to identify current theft trends and recommend improvements in loss prevention programs and staffing.
Administer systems and programs to reduce loss and maintain inventory control while training employees in operations and safety.
Prepare budgets and operational plans for wellness programs and fitness facilities while overseeing daily operations and site inspections.
Conduct needs assessments or surveys to determine interest in, or satisfaction with, wellness programs and organize health screenings.
